assertion_results.text: Output imports BunkerSigner and parseBunkerInput from @nostr/tools/nip46 (or nostr-tools/nip46)
assertion_results.evidence: import { type BunkerPointer, BunkerSigner, parseBunkerInput } from "@nostr/tools/nip46";

assertion_results.text: Output calls parseBunkerInput to parse the bunker:// URI
Output uses BunkerSigner.fromBunker() factory method (not constructor directly)
assertion_results.evidence: const signer = BunkerSigner.fromBunker(clientSecretKey, bunkerPointer, { pool, onauth: ... });

assertion_results.text: Output calls await bunker.connect() for the initial connection
Output calls await bunker.getPublicKey() after connecting and uses the returned value as the user identity
assertion_results.evidence: const userPubkey = await signer.getPublicKey(); — called after connect(), returned as part of BunkerConnection and stored in session as userPubkey

assertion_results.text: Output includes an onauth callback in BunkerSignerParams that handles auth challenge URLs
assertion_results.evidence: onauth: (url: string) => { if (onAuth) { onAuth(url); } else { window.open(url, "bunker-auth", "width=600,height=400"); } }

assertion_results.text: Output demonstrates session persistence by storing BunkerPointer and client secret key, and shows reconnection without calling connect() again
assertion_results.evidence: saveSession stores clientSecretKeyHex, bunkerPointer, and userPubkey to localStorage. reconnectFromSession() recreates BunkerSigner with stored data and comment says: '// No connect() call needed — the signer already recognizes this client.'

assertion_results.text: Output correctly distinguishes between remote-signer-pubkey (transport) and user-pubkey (identity)
assertion_results.evidence: CRITICAL: The remote-signer-pubkey (from the bunker:// URI) is the transport key. The user's actual identity pubkey may be DIFFERENT. Always call get_public_key after connecting to learn the real identity.
